attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- shadcn: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples. Applies when working with shadcn/ui, component registries, presets, --preset codes, or any project with a components.json file. Also triggers for "shadcn init", "create an app with --preset", or "switch to --preset".
- supabase-postgres-best-practices: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase. Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or optimizing Postgres queries, schema designs, or database configurations.
- supabase: Use when doing ANY task involving Supabase. Triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, getSession, getUser, getClaims, RLS); Supabase CLI or MCP server; schema changes, migrations, security audits, Postgres extensions (pg_graphql, pg_cron, pg_vector).
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Keine TypeScript-Fehler. Die VFS-Ansicht ist jetzt auf das Ordner-Grid-Design umgestellt:

- **2-spaltige Ordner-Kacheln** mit lila Gradient-Folder-Icon (CSS, kein SVG, also keine ID-Konflikte)
- **Farbige Quell-Badges** am unteren Rand der Ordner (Pro Typ eine farbige Kreismarke mit Initialen), max. 4 + "+N"
- **Kacheltext**: Sektion-Name fett + "Used by X entities"
- **Klick auf Ordner** → klappt die Entity-Liste darunter aus (gleiche `expandedPaths`-Logik wie vorher)
- Die gesamte Datenfetch-Logik und alles außerhalb der Render-Ausgabe bleibt unverändert

input.description: Show files changed in the api-layer interface commit

content.content:
commit c8bbbf3cce2e6d91df1307bf1a008df9b70a751c
Author: lassejohannis <lasse.johannis@thinc.de>
Date:   Sat Apr 25 22:47:43 2026 +0200

    feat(api-layer): #14 Schnittstelle für Software & AI
    
    REST endpoints:
      • POST /api/facts/{id}/validate|flag|edit (bi-temporal supersede)
      • GET  /api/graph/neighborhood/{id}?depth=…
      • POST /api/admin/webhooks (HMAC-signed outbound, 4 event types)
    
    Auth & rate limiting:
      • Supabase JWT (HS256) + agent_tokens (qx_<id>_<secret>, bcrypt)
      • Strict mode + API_AUTH_DISABLED dev bypass
      • Scope-aware (read/write/admin) on /api/* and /mcp/sse
      • slowapi: 60/min IP, 600/min token
    
    Pagination & event bus:
      • Opaque cursor helper (api/_pagination.py)
      • LISTEN/NOTIFY worker (sync/webhook_dispatcher.py)
      • migration 011_api_layer: agent_tokens, pending_types, webhooks,
        webhook_deliveries, fact_changes/entities NOTIFY triggers
    
    Tooling & docs:
      • CLI: token issue/list/revoke, mcp-stdio
      • Bruno collection (docs/api/qontext-bruno/)
      • Consumer guide (docs/api-consumer-guide.md)
      • GDPR demo script (scripts/gdpr_delete_demo.sh)
      • End-to-end smoke (scripts/smoke_api_layer.py — 27/27 green)

content.text:
**Lasse** (`lassejohannis`) hat das Context-Layer-Interface geändert — Commit `c8bbbf3` vom 25. April um 22:47.

Die Änderungen umfassten:
- **Neue REST-Endpoints**: `POST /api/facts/{id}/validate|flag|edit`, `GET /api/graph/neighborhood/{id}`, `POST /api/admin/webhooks`
- **Auth & Rate Limiting**: Supabase JWT + eigene `agent_tokens`, Scopes (read/write/admin), slowapi
- **Pagination + Event Bus**: Cursor-Helper, LISTEN/NOTIFY Worker, Webhook-Dispatcher
- **Migration 011**: neue Tabellen `agent_tokens`, `webhooks`, `webhook_deliveries`, NOTIFY-Trigger

git push --force origin Paul

content.text:
Der Commit `1fd9642` hat zwei Bereiche verändert:

**Server (`server/`)** — das willst du wahrscheinlich nicht:
- `api/admin.py`, `api/resolutions.py`, `api/vfs.py` — Backend-Endpoints
- `ontology/engine.py` — Ontologie-Logik
- `cli.py` — CLI-Änderungen

**Web (`web/`)** — das ist der Context Layer Frontend:
- `pages/browse/` — VfsTree, EntityDetail, ActionPanel (die Hauptansicht)
- `pages/search/` — SearchPage + searchLogic
- `pages/review/` — ConflictDetail, ConflictInbox
- `pages/workflow/` — WorkflowPage
- `components/entity/` — FactRow, ConfidencePill, EntityTypeBadge (shared components)
- `hooks/` — useEntity, useConflicts, useFact
- `lib/api.ts` — API-Client

**Die Frage ist**: Die Server-Änderungen in `1fd9642` sind wahrscheinlich nötig damit das Frontend funktioniert (z.B. `api/vfs.py` liefert die Daten für VfsTree). Wenn du nur die `web/`-Dateien nach main pushst aber `main` die passenden Server-Endpoints nicht hat, wird das Frontend ins Leere laufen.

**Mein Vorschlag**: Einen neuen Branch von main anlegen, dort alle `web/`-Dateien **und** die drei Server-API-Dateien (`api/admin.py`, `api/resolutions.py`, `api/vfs.py`) aus `1fd9642` einchecken und als PR nach main öffnen. Soll ich das so machen?

import { createBrowserRouter, RouterProvider, Navigate } from 'react-router-dom'
import AppShell from '@/components/layout/AppShell'
import BrowsePage from '@/pages/browse/BrowsePage'
import SearchPage from '@/pages/search/SearchPage'
import ReviewPage from '@/pages/review/ReviewPage'
import WorkflowPage from '@/pages/workflow/WorkflowPage'

const router = createBrowserRouter([
  {
    path: '/',
    element: <AppShell />,
    children: [
      { index: true, element: <Navigate to="/browse" replace /> },
      { path: 'browse', element: <BrowsePage /> },
      { path: 'browse/:entityId', element: <BrowsePage /> },
      { path: 'search', element: <SearchPage /> },
      { path: 'review', element: <ReviewPage /> },
      { path: 'review/:conflictId', element: <ReviewPage /> },
      { path: 'workflow', element: <WorkflowPage /> },
    ],
  },
])

export default function App() {
  return <RouterProvider router={router} />
}

Ich habe alles, was ich brauche. Plan:

1. **AppShell** → TopNav raus, linke Sidebar mit den Nav-Tabs rein
2. **BrowsePage** → kein ThreeColumnLayout mehr; Mitte = Folder-Grid, Rechts = Artefakt-Panel das aufklappt
3. **VfsTree** → wird das Center-Content: "Context vaults" Header, 3-spaltige Ordner, Drill-in zu Dateien

Ich schreibe alle drei Dateien auf einmal.

content.text:
Keine Fehler. Zusammenfassung der Änderungen:

**AppShell** — TopNav weg, feste linke Sidebar (220px) mit Logo + Browse/Search/Review/Graph als vertikale Nav-Items.

**BrowsePage** — Kein ThreeColumnLayout mehr. Links das Folder-Grid (flex-1), rechts ein Artefakt-Panel (400px) das per CSS-Transition rein-/rausgleitet wenn eine Entity ausgewählt/abgewählt wird. Oben ein X-Button zum Schließen.

**VfsTree** — Wird zum Center-Content:
- "Context vaults" als H1-Heading mit Breadcrumb wenn ein Ordner offen ist
- **3-spaltige Folder-Kacheln** mit lila Gradient-Folder-Illustration + Quell-Badges
- **Klick auf Ordner** → Drill-in, zeigt Entities als File-Rows mit Icon, Name, Typ-Badge und Hover-Chevron
- **Klick auf File** → öffnet das rechte Artefakt-Panel mit EntityDetail + ActionPanel
- Wenn eine Entity bereits aktiv ist beim Laden, öffnet sich der passende Ordner automatisch

# CODING AGENTS: READ THIS FIRST

This is a **handoff bundle** from Claude Design (claude.ai/design).

A user mocked up designs in HTML/CSS/JS using an AI design tool, then exported this bundle so a coding agent can implement the designs for real.

## What you should do — IMPORTANT

**Read the chat transcripts first.** There are 1 chat transcript(s) in `bigberlin/chats/`. The transcripts show the full back-and-forth between the user and the design assistant — they tell you **what the user actually wants** and **where they landed** after iterating. Don't skip them. The final HTML files are the output, but the chat is where the intent lives.

**Read `bigberlin/project/Browse Redesign.html` in full.** The user had this file open when they triggered the handoff, so it's almost certainly the primary design they want built. Read it top to bottom — don't skim. Then **follow its imports**: open every file it pulls in (shared components, CSS, scripts) so you understand how the pieces fit together before you start implementing.

**If anything is ambiguous, ask the user to confirm before you start implementing.** It's much cheaper to clarify scope up front than to build the wrong thing.

## About the design files

The design medium is **HTML/CSS/JS** — these are prototypes, not production code. Your job is to **recreate them pixel-perfectly** in whatever technology makes sense for the target codebase (React, Vue, native, whatever fits). Match the visual output; don't copy the prototype's internal structure unless it happens to fit.

**Don't render these files in a browser or take screenshots unless the user asks you to.** Everything you need — dimensions, colors, layout rules — is spelled out in the source. Read the HTML and CSS directly; a screenshot won't tell you anything they don't.

## Bundle contents

- `bigberlin/README.md` — this file
- `bigberlin/chats/` — conversation transcripts (read these!)
- `bigberlin/project/` — the `BigBerlin` project files (HTML prototypes, assets, components)

- **Ordner**: Kleines Apple-Finder-Style Grid — Folder-Icon + Label + Anzahl, kein überdimensioniertes Card mehr
- **Rechtes Panel immer sichtbar** — feste 272px Breite, zeigt Placeholder wenn nichts selektiert, füllt sich mit Entity-Details beim Klick

content.text:
Keine Fehler. Zusammenfassung was ich gebaut habe (exakt nach dem Design-File):

**AppShell** — Flat sidebar (196px, `#fafafa`) mit Qontext-Logo und Browse/Search/Review/Graph als einfache Nav-Items, kein Highlight-Icon.

**BrowsePage** — Dreiteiliges Layout:
- Mitte: VfsTree + Chat-Stub am Boden (Placeholder für das spätere Chat-Interface)
- Rechts: Info-Panel (280px, **immer sichtbar**) — bei keiner Auswahl zeigt es einen Placeholder, bei Auswahl EntityDetail + ActionPanel

**VfsTree** — Komplett nach dem Prototype:
- Topbar (52px) mit "Context vaults" / Breadcrumb wenn Ordner offen
- **Ordner-Grid** (`repeat(auto-fill, minmax(130px, 1fr))`) — kompakt wie Finder, jeder Typ in eigener Farbe mit echtem 3D-Folder-SVG (Back-Layer + Tab + Front mit Shine + Drop-Shadow)
- Farbige Quell-Dots (keine Buchstaben, per Design) unten links auf dem Ordner
- **Klick auf Ordner** → Finder-Listenansicht mit Spalten-Header (Name / Typ), staggered `rowIn`-Animationen pro Zeile
- **Klick auf Datei** → öffnet rechts die Entity-Details, aktive Zeile farblich hervorgehoben mit Chevron

content.content:
1	import type { Entity, SearchResultItem } from '../../types'
2	
3	export type FilterConstraint = { op: 'gt' | 'gte'; value: number } | null
4	
5	export interface CascadeInterpretation {
6	  titleTerms: string[]
7	  wantsTasks: boolean
8	  wantsTickets: boolean
9	  wantsConnections: boolean
10	  wantsPeople: boolean
11	  ticketConstraint: FilterConstraint
12	  connectionConstraint: FilterConstraint
13	  warnings: string[]
14	}
15	
16	const TITLE_PATTERN =
17	  /(senior|lead|principal|staff|junior)?\s*(engineer|developer|manager|director|architect|analyst|consultant)/gi
18	
19	const TICKET_PREDICATES = [
20	  'raised_ticket',
21	  'assigned_ticket',
22	  'owns_ticket',
23	  'ticket',
24	  'incident',
25	]
26	
27	const CONNECTION_EXCLUDE_PREDICATES = [
28	  ...TICKET_PREDICATES,
29	  'email',
30	  'phone',
31	  'address',
32	  'url',
33	  'website',
34	]
35	
36	const PEOPLE_TYPES = new Set(['person'])
37	const TASK_TYPES = new Set(['task', 'project', 'process'])
38	const TICKET_FOCUS_TYPES = new Set(['ticket', 'communication', 'person'])
39	const CONNECTION_TERMS = '(?:connections?|contacts?|kontakte?|verbindungen?|netzwerk)'
40	const TICKET_TERMS = '(?:tickets?|issues?)'
41	
42	function asText(value: unknown): string {
43	  if (value == null) return ''
44	  if (typeof value === 'string') return value
45	  if (typeof value === 'number' || typeof value === 'boolean') return String(value)
46	  if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.map((v) => asText(v)).join(' ')
47	  if (typeof value === 'object') {
48	    return Object.values(value as Record<string, unknown>)
49	      .map((v) => asText(v))
50	      .join(' ')
51	  }
52	  return ''
53	}
54	
55	function parseIntegerToken(raw: string | undefined): number | null {
56	  if (!raw) return null
57	  const digits = raw.replace(/[^\d]/g, '')
58	  if (!digits) return null
59	  const value = Number.parseInt(digits, 10)
60	  return Number.isFinite(value) ? value : null
61	}
62	
63	function extractConstraint(query: string, termPattern: string): FilterConstraint {
64	  const patterns: Array<{ op: 'gt' | 'gte'; re: RegExp }> = [
65	    { op: 'gt', re: new RegExp(`(?:mehr\\s+als|ueber|über|over|>)\\s*([\\d.,_\\s]+)\\s*${termPattern}`, 'i') },
66	    { op: 'gte', re: new RegExp(`(?:mindestens|at\\s+least|>=|mind\\.)\\s*([\\d.,_\\s]+)\\s*${termPattern}`, 'i') },
67	    { op: 'gt', re: new RegExp(`${termPattern}\\s*(?:mehr\\s+als|ueber|über|over|>)\\s*([\\d.,_\\s]+)`, 'i') },
68	    { op: 'gte', re: new RegExp(`${termPattern}\\s*(?:mindestens|at\\s+least|>=|mind\\.)\\s*([\\d.,_\\s]+)`, 'i') },
69	    { op: 'gte', re: new RegExp(`([\\d.,_\\s]+)\\s*\\+\\s*${termPattern}`, 'i') },
70	    { op: 'gte', re: new RegExp(`${termPattern}\\s*([\\d.,_\\s]+)\\s*\\+`, 'i') },
71	  ]
72	
73	  for (const pattern of patterns) {
74	    const match = query.match(pattern.re)
75	    const value = parseIntegerToken(match?.[1])
76	    if (value == null) continue
77	    return { op: pattern.op, value }
78	  }
79	
80	  return null
81	}
82	
83	function hasUnsupportedNegation(q: string): boolean {
84	  return /\b(ohne|kein|keine|keinen|nicht|weniger\s+als|at\s+most|max(?:imal)?|<=|<|under)\b/i.test(q)
85	}
86	
87	function getEntityText(entity: Entity): string {
88	  return [
89	    entity.canonical_name,
90	    entity.aliases.join(' '),
91	    asText(entity.attrs),
92	  ]
93	    .join(' ')
94	    .toLowerCase()
95	}
96	
97	export function parseInterpretation(query: string): CascadeInterpretation {
98	  const q = query.toLowerCase()
99	
100	  const titleMatches = [...query.matchAll(TITLE_PATTERN)]
101	  const titleTerms = titleMatches
102	    .flatMap((m) => [m[1], m[2]])
103	    .filter((part): part is string => !!part)
104	    .map((part) => part.toLowerCase())
105	
106	  const ticketConstraint = extractConstraint(query, TICKET_TERMS)
107	  const connectionConstraint = extractConstraint(query, CONNECTION_TERMS)
108	
109	  const warnings: string[] = []
110	  if (hasUnsupportedNegation(q) && (/\b(tickets?|incidents?|issues?)\b/i.test(q) || /\b(connection|connections|contact|contacts|kontakt|kontakte|verbindung|verbindungen|netzwerk)\b/i.test(q))) {
111	    warnings.push('Negationen wie "ohne", "weniger als" oder "<=" werden aktuell nur eingeschränkt unterstützt.')
112	  }
113	
114	  return {
115	    titleTerms,
116	    wantsTasks: /\b(workstream|task|todo|to-do|schritt|steps?)\b/i.test(q),
117	    wantsTickets: /\b(tickets?|incidents?|issues?)\b/i.test(q),
118	    wantsConnections: /\b(connection|connections|kontakt|kontakte|verbindung|verbindungen|netzwerk)\b/i.test(q),
119	    wantsPeople: /\b(person|personen|people|employee|employees|mitarbeiter)\b/i.test(q),
120	    ticketConstraint,
121	    connectionConstraint,
122	    warnings,
123	  }
124	}
125	
126	export function getTicketCount(entity: Entity): number {
127	  return entity.facts.filter((fact) => {
128	    const predicate = String(fact.predicate || '').toLowerCase()
129	    if (TICKET_PREDICATES.some((token) => predicate.includes(token))) return true
130	
131	    if (fact.object_literal == null) return false
132	    const literal = String(fact.object_literal).toLowerCase()
133	    return literal.includes('ticket') || literal.startsWith('inc-') || literal.startsWith('req-')
134	  }).length
135	}
136	
137	export function getConnectionCount(entity: Entity): number {
138	  return new Set(
139	    entity.facts
140	      .filter((fact) => {
141	        if (!fact.object_id) return false
142	        const predicate = String(fact.predicate || '').toLowerCase()
143	        return !CONNECTION_EXCLUDE_PREDICATES.some((token) => predicate.includes(token))
144	      })
145	      .map((fact) => fact.object_id)
146	      .filter((id): id is string => typeof id === 'string' && id.length > 0),
147	  ).size
148	}
149	
150	export function cascadeFilter(results: SearchResultItem[], intent: CascadeInterpretation): { filtered: SearchResultItem[]; steps: string[] } {
151	  let filtered = [...results]
152	  const steps: string[] = []
153	
154	  steps.push(`Hybrid Search geliefert: ${results.length} Kandidaten`)
155	
156	  if (intent.wantsPeople || intent.titleTerms.length > 0 || intent.wantsConnections || intent.connectionConstraint) {
157	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => PEOPLE_TYPES.has(String(result.entity.entity_type).toLowerCase()))
158	    steps.push(`Personen-Fokus: ${filtered.length} übrig`)
159	  }
160	
161	  if (intent.titleTerms.length > 0) {
162	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => {
163	      const text = getEntityText(result.entity)
164	      return intent.titleTerms.every((term) => text.includes(term))
165	    })
166	    steps.push(`Titel-Filter (${intent.titleTerms.join(' + ')}): ${filtered.length} übrig`)
167	  }
168	
169	  if (intent.ticketConstraint) {
170	    const constraint = intent.ticketConstraint
171	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => {
172	      const count = getTicketCount(result.entity)
173	      if (constraint.op === 'gt') return count > constraint.value
174	      return count >= constraint.value
175	    })
176	    const opLabel = constraint.op === 'gt' ? '>' : '≥'
177	    steps.push(`Ticket-Filter (${opLabel} ${constraint.value}): ${filtered.length} übrig`)
178	  }
179	
180	  if (intent.connectionConstraint) {
181	    const constraint = intent.connectionConstraint
182	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => {
183	      const count = getConnectionCount(result.entity)
184	      if (constraint.op === 'gt') return count > constraint.value
185	      return count >= constraint.value
186	    })
187	    const opLabel = constraint.op === 'gt' ? '>' : '≥'
188	    steps.push(`Connections-Filter (${opLabel} ${constraint.value}): ${filtered.length} übrig`)
189	  }
190	
191	  if (intent.wantsTasks && !intent.wantsTickets) {
192	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => TASK_TYPES.has(String(result.entity.entity_type).toLowerCase()))
193	    steps.push(`Workstream/Task-Fokus: ${filtered.length} übrig`)
194	  }
195	
196	  if (intent.wantsTickets && intent.titleTerms.length === 0 && !intent.ticketConstraint) {
197	    filtered = filtered.filter((result) => TICKET_FOCUS_TYPES.has(String(result.entity.entity_type).toLowerCase()))
198	    steps.push(`Ticket-Fokus: ${filtered.length} übrig`)
199	  }
200	
201	  for (const warning of intent.warnings) {
202	    steps.push(`Hinweis: ${warning}`)
203	  }
204	
205	  filtered.sort((a, b) => {
206	    if (intent.wantsConnections || intent.connectionConstraint) {
207	      const connectionDelta = getConnectionCount(b.entity) - getConnectionCount(a.entity)
208	      if (connectionDelta !== 0) return connectionDelta
209	    }
210	    const ticketDelta = getTicketCount(b.entity) - getTicketCount(a.entity)
211	    if (ticketDelta !== 0) return ticketDelta
212	    return b.score - a.score
213	  })
214	
215	  return { filtered, steps }
216	}
217	
218	export function describeResult(entity: Entity): string {
219	  const role = asText(entity.attrs).trim()
220	  const ticketCount = getTicketCount(entity)
221	  const connectionCount = getConnectionCount(entity)
222	  if (role) {
223	    return `${entity.canonical_name} · ${String(entity.entity_type)} · ${connectionCount} Connections · ${ticketCount} Tickets · ${role.slice(0, 90)}`
224	  }
225	  return `${entity.canonical_name} · ${String(entity.entity_type)} · ${connectionCount} Connections · ${ticketCount} Tickets`
226	}

// Source dot colors per type (no letters – just colored dots as per design)
const TYPE_SOURCE_DOTS: Record<string, string[]> = {
  person:        ['#611f69', '#ff7a59', '#EA4335'],
  company:       ['#ff7a59', '#000000', '#EA4335'],
  email:         ['#EA4335', '#4285F4'],
  communication: ['#EA4335', '#611f69'],

  task:          ['#0052cc', '#611f69', '#000000'],

  policy:        ['#000000'],
}

// ── Folder SVG illustration ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

function FolderSVG({ type }: { type: string }) {
  const pal = PALETTE[type] ?? PALETTE.document
  const [back, fl, fd] = pal
  const id = `fold-${type}`
  return (
    <svg viewBox="0 0 200 130" fill="none" style={{ width: '100%', display: 'block' }}>
      <defs>
        <linearGradient id={`${id}-front`} x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
          <stop offset="0%" stopColor={fl} />
          <stop offset="100%" stopColor={fd} />
        </linearGradient>
        <linearGradient id={`${id}-back`} x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
          <stop offset="0%" stopColor={back} stopOpacity="0.9" />
          <stop offset="100%" stopColor={back} stopOpacity="0.7" />
        </linearGradient>
        <filter id={`${id}-shadow`}>
          <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="4" stdDeviation="6" floodColor={fd} floodOpacity="0.35" />
        </filter>
        <filter id={`${id}-backshadow`}>
          <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="2" stdDeviation="3" floodColor={back} floodOpacity="0.3" />
        </filter>
      </defs>
      {/* Back layer */}
      <g filter={`url(#${id}-backshadow)`}>
        <path d="M18 38 Q18 28 28 28 H72 Q78 28 80 34 L86 42 H18 Z" fill={`url(#${id}-back)`} />
        <rect x="14" y="40" width="172" height="76" rx="10" fill={`url(#${id}-back)`} />
      </g>
      {/* Front layer */}
      <g filter={`url(#${id}-shadow)`}>
        <rect x="6" y="50" width="172" height="72" rx="10" fill={`url(#${id}-front)`} />
        <rect x="6" y="50" width="172" height="24" rx="10" fill="white" opacity="0.18" />
        <rect x="6" y="50" width="172" height="72" rx="10" stroke="white" strokeWidth="1" strokeOpacity="0.2" fill="none" />
      </g>
    </svg>
  )
}

// ── Source dot badges ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function SourceDots({ type }: { type: string }) {
  const colors = TYPE_SOURCE_DOTS[type.toLowerCase()] ?? ['#888']
  const visible = colors.slice(0, 3)
  const extra = colors.length > 3 ? colors.length - 3 : 0
  return (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', marginTop: 8 }}>
      {visible.map((color, i) => (
        <div key={i} style={{
          width: 18, height: 18, borderRadius: '50%',
          background: color, border: '2px solid white',
          marginLeft: i === 0 ? 0 : -5,
          zIndex: visible.length - i, position: 'relative',
        }} />
      ))}
      {extra > 0 && (
        <div style={{
          width: 18, height: 18, borderRadius: '50%',
          background: '#e8e8e8', border: '2px solid white',
          marginLeft: -5, display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center',

content.text:
Keine Fehler. Hier was sich geändert hat:

**Ordner** — Die farbigen Punkte sind komplett weg. Die Ordner-Illustration ist jetzt clean ohne Badges unten.

**Chat** — Unten in Browse ist jetzt der vollständige Search-Chat eingebaut (gleiche Logik wie SearchPage mit Graph-RAG, Cascade-Filter, etc.):
- Input immer sichtbar
- Mit Enter oder Button senden
- Nachrichten-History klappt sich automatisch auf sobald man sendet
- Toggle-Button (▴/▾ + Anzahl) zum Ein-/Ausklappen
- Ergebnisse erscheinen als klickbare Entity-Karten

**Auto-Navigation** — Wenn man auf ein Ergebnis klickt (`navigate('/browse/{entityId}')`):
1. URL wechselt zu `/browse/:entityId`
2. VfsTree bemerkt den neuen `selectedEntityId`
3. `useEffect` findet den richtigen Ordner und öffnet ihn automatisch
4. Die Datei-Zeile wird farblich hervorgehoben mit Chevron
5. Rechts öffnet sich das Info-Panel mit allen Entity-Details

content.text:
Sauber. Die drei Änderungen:

**1. Rechtes Panel** — von 280px auf **380px** verbreitert. Das Zentrum schrumpft entsprechend (flex-1 mit minWidth 0 damit es nicht überläuft).

**2. Resizable Chat** — Der Chat-Bereich hat oben einen **Drag-Handle** (graue Pille). Raufziehen = Chat wird größer (bis 480px), runterziehen = Chat kollabiert auf die Input-Bar (56px). Der Toggle-Button (▴/▾) klappt alternativ auf 240px.

**3. Eindeutige Suchergebnisse** — Wenn nach dem Cascade-Filter **genau 1 Treffer** übrig bleibt:
- Direkte Navigation zu `/browse/{entityId}` → VfsTree öffnet automatisch den richtigen Ordner, Datei wird highlighted
- Im Chat erscheint nur kurz `→ „Name" gefunden — direkt geöffnet.` — keine Karten-Liste
- Bei mehreren Treffern weiterhin die klickbare Liste wie bisher

87	        entity_row = e_res.data
88	    except Exception as exc:
89	        if _is_single_row_not_found(exc):
90	            return None
91	        raise
92	    if not entity_row:
93	        return None
94	
95	    facts_q = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("subject_id", entity_id)
96	    if as_of:
97	        facts_q = facts_q.lte("valid_from", as_of.isoformat()).or_(
98	            f"valid_to.is.null,valid_to.gte.{as_of.isoformat()}"
99	        )
100	    else:
101	        facts_q = facts_q.is_("valid_to", "null")
102	    facts = facts_q.execute().data or []
103	
104	    try:
105	        trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
106	        trust_row = trust_res.data
107	    except Exception as exc:

109	            trust_row = None
110	        else:
111	            raise
112	    return _build_entity_response(entity_row, trust_row, facts)
113	
114	
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# Hybrid search implementation
117	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	
119	def run_hybrid_search(query: str, k: int = 10, as_of: datetime | None = None, entity_type: str | None = None, db=None) -> list[SearchResult]:
120	    if db is None:
121	        db = get_db()
122	
123	    # --- Stage 1: Semantic ---
124	    semantic_scores: dict[str, float] = {}
125	    try:
126	        query_embedding = embed_text(query, dimensions=768)
127	        rpc_params: dict = {
128	            "query_embedding": query_embedding,
129	            "match_threshold": 0.65,
130	            "match_count": min(k * 2, 40),
131	            "use_inference_embedding": True,
132	        }
133	        sem_res = db.rpc("match_entities", rpc_params).execute()
134	        for row in sem_res.data or []:
135	            if entity_type and row.get("entity_type") != entity_type:
136	                continue
137	            semantic_scores[str(row["id"])] = float(row["similarity"])
138	    except Exception as exc:
139	        logger.warning("Semantic search failed (embedding unavailable?): %s", exc)
140	
141	    # --- Stage 2: Structural ---
142	    structural_ids: set[str] = set()
143	    mentions = _extract_mentions(query)
144	    for mention in mentions[:5]:  # cap at 5 mentions
145	        name_res = db.table("entities").select("id").ilike(
146	            "canonical_name", f"%{mention}%"
147	        ).limit(3).execute()
148	        for row in name_res.data or []:
149	            root_id = str(row["id"])
150	            structural_ids.add(root_id)
151	            # 1-hop graph traverse
152	            hop_res = db.table("facts").select("subject_id, object_id").or_(
153	                f"subject_id.eq.{root_id},object_id.eq.{root_id}"
154	            ).is_("valid_to", "null").limit(20).execute()
155	            for f in hop_res.data or []:
156	                if f.get("subject_id"):
157	                    structural_ids.add(str(f["subject_id"]))
158	                if f.get("object_id"):
159	                    structural_ids.add(str(f["object_id"]))
160	
161	    # --- Stage 3: Combine + rerank ---
162	    if semantic_scores and structural_ids:
163	        intersection = set(semantic_scores.keys()) & structural_ids
164	        combined_ids = intersection if intersection else (set(semantic_scores.keys()) | structural_ids)
165	        match_type = "hybrid"
166	    elif semantic_scores:
167	        combined_ids = set(semantic_scores.keys())
168	        match_type = "semantic"
169	    else:
170	        combined_ids = structural_ids
171	        match_type = "structural"
172	
173	    results: list[SearchResult] = []
174	    for entity_id in list(combined_ids)[:k * 2]:
175	        entity = _fetch_entity_with_trust(db, entity_id, as_of)
176	        if not entity:
177	            continue
178	        if entity_type and entity.entity_type != entity_type:
179	            continue
180	        score = semantic_scores.get(entity_id, 0.5)
181	        # Boost by trust score
182	        final_score = score * 0.7 + entity.trust_score * 0.3
183	        results.append(SearchResult(entity=entity, score=final_score, match_type=match_type))
184	
185	    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.score, reverse=True)
186	    return results[:k]
187	
188	
189	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	# Router
191	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
192	
193	@router.post("/search", response_model=SearchResponse)
194	def search(req: SearchRequest, db=Depends(get_db)):
195	    if not req.query.strip():
196	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Query must not be empty")
197	
198	    results = run_hybrid_search(
199	        query=req.query,
200	        k=req.k,
201	        as_of=req.as_of,
202	        entity_type=req.entity_type,
203	        db=db,
204	    )
205	    return SearchResponse(query=req.query, results=results, total=len(results))
206

file.content:
"""POST /search — 3-Stage Hybrid Search: Semantic ∩ Structural → Rerank.

Stage 1 — Semantic: pgvector kNN on inference_embedding (fallback: name_embedding)
Stage 2 — Structural: extract entity mentions → 1-hop graph traverse
Stage 3 — Combine (intersect if both have hits, union otherwise) + rerank
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
from datetime import datetime

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException

from server.db import get_db, embed_text
from server.models import (
    EntityResponse, FactResponse,
    SearchRequest, SearchResponse, SearchResult,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
router = APIRouter(tags=["search"])


def _is_single_row_not_found(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    text = str(exc)
    return "PGRST116" in text or "multiple (or no) rows returned" in text


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity mention extraction (Pioneer stub → naive regex fallback)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _extract_mentions(query: str) -> list[str]:
    """Return candidate entity name mentions from a query string.

    Pioneer fine-tune is wired here when WS-3 ships (replace this stub).
    Fallback: capitalised word sequences (≥2 chars, ≥1 word).
    """
    try:
        from server.extractors.pioneer import extract_mentions  # type: ignore
        return extract_mentions(query)
    except (ImportError, Exception):
        pass

    # Simple fallback: grab capitalised tokens or quoted strings
    quoted = re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', query)
    capitalised = re.findall(r'\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,}(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,})*)\b', query)
    return list(dict.fromkeys(quoted + capitalised))  # dedupe, preserve order


def _build_entity_response(entity_row: dict, trust_row: dict | None, facts: list[dict]) -> EntityResponse:
    fact_responses = [
        FactResponse(
            id=str(f["id"]),
            subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
            predicate=f["predicate"],
            object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
            object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
            confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
            derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
            extraction_method=f.get("extraction_method"),
            valid_from=f["valid_from"],
            valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
            recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
            source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
        )
        for f in facts
    ]
    return EntityResponse(
        id=str(entity_row["id"]),
        entity_type=entity_row["entity_type"],
        canonical_name=entity_row["canonical_name"],
        aliases=entity_row.get("aliases") or [],
        attrs=entity_row.get("attrs") or {},
        trust_score=float((trust_row or {}).get("trust_score") or 0.0),
        fact_count=int((trust_row or {}).get("fact_count") or 0),
        source_diversity=int((trust_row or {}).get("source_diversity") or 0),
        facts=fact_responses,
    )


def _fetch_entity_with_trust(db, entity_id: str, as_of: datetime | None = None) -> EntityResponse | None:
    try:
        e_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
        entity_row = e_res.data
    except Exception as exc:
        if _is_single_row_not_found(exc):
            return None
        raise
    if not entity_row:
        return None

    facts_q = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("subject_id", entity_id)
    if as_of:
        facts_q = facts_q.lte("valid_from", as_of.isoformat()).or_(
            f"valid_to.is.null,valid_to.gte.{as_of.isoformat()}"
        )
    else:
        facts_q = facts_q.is_("valid_to", "null")
    facts = facts_q.execute().data or []

    try:
        trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
        trust_row = trust_res.data
    except Exception as exc:
        if _is_single_row_not_found(exc):
            trust_row = None
        else:
            raise
    return _build_entity_response(entity_row, trust_row, facts)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hybrid search implementation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def run_hybrid_search(query: str, k: int = 10, as_of: datetime | None = None, entity_type: str | None = None, db=None) -> list[SearchResult]:
    if db is None:
        db = get_db()

    # --- Stage 1: Semantic ---
    semantic_scores: dict[str, float] = {}
    try:
        query_embedding = embed_text(query, dimensions=768)
        rpc_params: dict = {
            "query_embedding": query_embedding,
            "match_threshold": 0.65,
            "match_count": min(k * 2, 40),
            "use_inference_embedding": True,
        }
        sem_res = db.rpc("match_entities", rpc_params).execute()
        for row in sem_res.data or []:
            if entity_type and row.get("entity_type") != entity_type:
                continue
            semantic_scores[str(row["id"])] = float(row["similarity"])
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.warning("Semantic search failed (embedding unavailable?): %s", exc)

    # --- Stage 2: Structural ---
    structural_ids: set[str] = set()
    mentions = _extract_mentions(query)
    for mention in mentions[:5]:  # cap at 5 mentions
        name_res = db.table("entities").select("id").ilike(
            "canonical_name", f"%{mention}%"
        ).limit(3).execute()
        for row in name_res.data or []:
            root_id = str(row["id"])
            structural_ids.add(root_id)
            # 1-hop graph traverse
            hop_res = db.table("facts").select("subject_id, object_id").or_(
                f"subject_id.eq.{root_id},object_id.eq.{root_id}"
            ).is_("valid_to", "null").limit(20).execute()
            for f in hop_res.data or []:
                if f.get("subject_id"):
                    structural_ids.add(str(f["subject_id"]))
                if f.get("object_id"):
                    structural_ids.add(str(f["object_id"]))

    # --- Stage 3: Combine + rerank ---
    if semantic_scores and structural_ids:
        intersection = set(semantic_scores.keys()) & structural_ids
        combined_ids = intersection if intersection else (set(semantic_scores.keys()) | structural_ids)
        match_type = "hybrid"
    elif semantic_scores:
        combined_ids = set(semantic_scores.keys())
        match_type = "semantic"
    else:
        combined_ids = structural_ids
        match_type = "structural"

    results: list[SearchResult] = []
    for entity_id in list(combined_ids)[:k * 2]:
        entity = _fetch_entity_with_trust(db, entity_id, as_of)
        if not entity:
            continue
        if entity_type and entity.entity_type != entity_type:
            continue
        score = semantic_scores.get(entity_id, 0.5)
        # Boost by trust score
        final_score = score * 0.7 + entity.trust_score * 0.3
        results.append(SearchResult(entity=entity, score=final_score, match_type=match_type))

    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.score, reverse=True)
    return results[:k]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Router
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@router.post("/search", response_model=SearchResponse)
def search(req: SearchRequest, db=Depends(get_db)):
    if not req.query.strip():
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Query must not be empty")

    results = run_hybrid_search(
        query=req.query,
        k=req.k,
        as_of=req.as_of,
        entity_type=req.entity_type,
        db=db,
    )
    return SearchResponse(query=req.query, results=results, total=len(results))

content.text: Die API selbst gibt 200 zurück wenn der Server läuft. Das 500 kommt wahrscheinlich wenn der Server nicht läuft oder die Embeddings fehlen. Lass mich das genauer prüfen.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session covered multiple requests for the BigBerlinHack/TechEurope "Qontext" project:

a) **VFS Browse redesign** (initial): Change the left-panel accordion VFS tree into a folder grid UI matching a reference screenshot (purple folder cards, source icon badges, "Used by X tools" text)

b) **Git operations**: Identify who changed the context layer interface on main (answer: `lassejohannis`, commit `c8bbbf3`), then reset the `Paul` branch to `1fd9642` (before the main merge), then create a PR with only `web/` files to main

c) **Design file implementation**: Fetch and implement a Claude Design export (`Browse Redesign.html`) pixel-perfectly, which specified:
   - macOS Finder-style compact folder grid with type-colored SVG folder illustrations
   - Left sidebar navigation replacing the top nav
   - Always-visible right info panel (280px)
   - Drill-in: click folder → Finder file list view
   - Chat stub at bottom (placeholder for future)

d) **Remove source dots**: Remove the colored circle badges from the bottom of folder cards entirely

e) **Integrate Search chat**: Add the full Search page chat (Graph-RAG with cascadeFilter) as a bottom panel in Browse, not just a placeholder

f) **Direct navigation for unique results**: When search returns exactly 1 result, navigate directly to the entity in the file system (open folder, highlight file) without showing entity cards in chat

g) **UI adjustments**: Right panel wider (280→380px), chat panel height-resizable via drag handle

h) **500 error investigation**: `/api/search` returning 500, investigate root cause

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui (web stack)
   - FastAPI + Pydantic v2 (server stack)
   - Supabase (Postgres + pgvector) for data
   - VFS (Virtual File System) — entity browser organized by domain sections fetched from `/api/vfs/_sections` and `/api/vfs/{pluralType}`
   - Hybrid search: pgvector semantic + structural graph traversal (`/api/search`)
   - `cascadeFilter` / `parseInterpretation` from `searchLogic.ts` — NLP-based query parsing
   - SVG gradient IDs must be unique per folder type to avoid conflicts in DOM
   - CSS keyframe animations: `fadeUp` (.22s) and `rowIn` (.14s staggered) for folder/file transitions
   - `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` required for git operations and persistent file writes
   - React mouse drag pattern: `document.addEventListener('mousemove'/'mouseup')` for resize
   - Git reset —hard + force push pattern for branch rollback

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`web/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx`**
  - Changed from top-nav layout to sidebar nav layout
  - Note: linter has been reverting this file back to TopNav version repeatedly (system-reminders confirm)
  - Current committed state may be the OLD version with TopNav
  ```tsx
  // Target state (sidebar nav):
  const NAV_ITEMS = [
    { to: '/browse', label: 'Browse' },
    { to: '/search', label: 'Search' },
    { to: '/review', label: 'Review' },
    { to: '/workflow', label: 'Graph' },
  ]
  // 196px wide sidebar with #fafafa background, flat nav items, Database icon logo
  ```

- **`web/src/pages/browse/BrowsePage.tsx`**
  - Completely rewritten. Most recent version has:
    - `BrowseChat` component (full Search chat logic integrated)
    - Resizable chat panel with drag handle
    - 380px always-visible right info panel
    - Direct navigation for unique search results
  ```tsx
  // Key unique-result logic in handleSend:
  if (payload.matches.length === 1) {
    const single = payload.matches[0]
    navigate(`/browse/${encodeURIComponent(single.entity.id)}`)
    setMessages(prev => [...prev, {
      id: `a-${Date.now()}`, role: 'assistant',
      content: `→ „${single.entity.canonical_name}" gefunden — direkt geöffnet.`,
    }])
    return
  }

  // Drag-to-resize:
  const MIN_H = 56
  const MAX_H = 480
  function startResize(e: React.MouseEvent) {
    const startY = e.clientY; const startH = chatHeight
    function onMove(ev: MouseEvent) {
      setChatHeight(Math.max(MIN_H, Math.min(MAX_H, startH + (startY - ev.clientY))))
    }
    document.addEventListener('mousemove', onMove)
    document.addEventListener('mouseup', () => document.removeEventListener('mousemove', onMove))
  }

  // Right panel: width 380px, always visible
  <aside style={{ width: 380, flexShrink: 0, borderLeft: '1px solid #e5e5e5', ... }}>
  ```

- **`web/src/pages/browse/VfsTree.tsx`**
  - Complete rewrite as center content (not sidebar)
  - Folder grid with PALETTE-colored SVG folder illustrations
  - Source dots removed
  - Fixed auto-open useEffect:
  ```tsx
  const PALETTE: Record<string, [string, string, string, string]> = {
    person:        ['#1d4ed8', '#93c5fd', '#60a5fa', '#3b82f6'],
    company:       ['#c2410c', '#fdba74', '#fb923c', '#f97316'],
    document:      ['#334155', '#94a3b8', '#64748b', '#64748b'],
    event:         ['#6d28d9', '#c4b5fd', '#a78bfa', '#8b5cf6'],
    task:          ['#065f46', '#6ee7b7', '#34d399', '#10b981'],
    email:         ['#991b1b', '#fca5a5', '#f87171', '#ef4444'],
    policy:        ['#9d174d', '#f9a8d4', '#f472b6', '#ec4899'],
    ticket:        ['#312e81', '#a5b4fc', '#818cf8', '#6366f1'],
    communication: ['#7c2d12', '#fdba74', '#fb923c', '#f97316'],
  }

  // FolderSVG with unique gradient IDs per type:
  function FolderSVG({ type }: { type: string }) {
    const id = `fold-${type}`
    // Back layer: path (tab) + rect with url(#${id}-back)
    // Front layer: rect with url(#${id}-front) + shine + inner border stroke
    // Filters: drop shadows
  }

  // Auto-open fixed (no openSection guard):
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!selectedEntityId) return
    const sec = sections.find(s => s.items.some(i => i.entityId === selectedEntityId))
    if (sec && sec.path !== openSection) setOpenSection(sec.path)
  }, [selectedEntityId, sections]) // openSection intentionally excluded

  // Folder grid: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(130px, 1fr)), gap 14px
  // File list: white card, column header (Name/Typ), FileRow with doc SVG icon
  ```

- **`web/src/index.css`**
  - Added CSS keyframe animations at end of file:
  ```css
  @keyframes fadeUp { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(10px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateY(0); } }
  @keyframes rowIn { from { opacity:0; transform:translateX(-6px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateX(0); } }
  .fade-up { animation: fadeUp .22s ease forwards; }
  .row-in  { animation: rowIn .14s ease forwards; opacity: 0; animation-fill-mode: forwards; }
  ```

- **`server/src/server/api/search.py`**
  - Read to investigate 500 error
  - 3-stage hybrid search: pgvector semantic → structural graph → combine+rerank
  - Uses `embed_text()` from `server.db` for query embedding
  - curl tests confirmed it returns 200 with valid results

- **`web/src/pages/search/SearchPage.tsx`** and **`searchLogic.ts`**
  - Read to understand chat logic for integration into Browse
  - `searchMemory` API call, `parseInterpretation`, `cascadeFilter`, `buildAssistantPayload`
  - Imported into BrowsePage as `'../search/searchLogic'`

4. Errors and fixes:

- **Linter repeatedly reverting files**: After writing AppShell.tsx, BrowsePage.tsx, VfsTree.tsx, the linter (system-reminder) kept reverting them to old accordion/TopNav versions. Fixed by using `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` on Write calls.

- **git reset didn't persist**: First `git reset --hard 1fd9642` ran in sandbox mode and had no effect. Fixed with `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true`.

- **`TYPE_SOURCE_DOTS` not found after partial deletion**: Removed the SourceDots usage in FolderCard but left the `SourceDots` function and `TYPE_SOURCE_DOTS` constant, causing TS error "declared but never read" and "cannot find name". Fixed by removing both the function and the constant.

- **TypeScript `ChevronDown` unused import**: After removing accordion, `ChevronDown` import was unused. Removed from imports.

- **`web/test` junk file**: When doing `git checkout 1fd9642 -- web/`, a `web/test` file with content "test" was included. Removed with `git restore --staged web/test && rm -f web/test`.

- **`FactResponse` status field**: Investigated whether `status`/`evidence` fields on API response could cause validation error. Found they ARE defined in `models.py` (line 61, 62).

5. Problem Solving:

- **Transformed Browse from 3-column layout to 2-column** (sidebar nav in AppShell + center content + right panel) matching the design file
- **Unified Search and Browse chat** by importing searchLogic.ts into BrowsePage and re-implementing the same Graph-RAG chat pattern
- **Auto-folder-open on navigation**: VfsTree's useEffect watches selectedEntityId and automatically opens the matching section, enabling the chat→navigate→highlight flow
- **500 error on /api/search**: curl tests to port 8000 return 200 with valid data. Issue is likely that the Vite dev server isn't proxying correctly, or the server isn't running when the user makes the request from the browser. The embedding code path (Gemini) may also fail without a valid API key. Investigation ongoing.

6. All user messages:
   - "schau dir die browse abteilung an und ändere das vfs zu so einem aussehen. [screenshot] Aber behalte alles andere gleich"
   - "schau auf main, wer das interface von dem context layer geändert hat"
   - "okay kannst du das wieder zurück machen auf mein letzten Stand auf Paul vor ich main rein gemerged habe"
   - "ja aber nur auf der Paul branch. Also nicht auf main. So das ich auf Paul wieder die alte Version habe"
   - "ja" (confirming git reset)
   - "okay wo ist mein stand nun?"
   - "mach du es" (for git reset with dangerouslyDisableSandbox)
   - "okay ja das ist der commit den ich will. Allerdings will ich davon nur das frontend für den context layer. Wie kann ich nun nur diesen Teil wieder in main pushen?"
   - "was meinst du? Geh mehr ins detail"
   - "nein, bitte füge auf main nur die web/ dateien von diesem branch hin. also nichts server seitige"
   - "[screenshot] aktuell hast du so ein Interface. Allerdings will ich das es geändert wird, also dass in der Mitte Ordner sind, quasi wie in Apple so ein Ordner struktur. Hier ist ein Bild. Außerdem füge dann in die linke Spalte die Tabs von oben ein. Und wenn man ein Ordner öffnet sollen dann die Dateien angezeigt werden. Wenn man dann die Datei öffnet/ interagiert soll sich rechts ein Artefakt öffnen..."
   - "Fetch this design file, read its readme, and implement the relevant aspects of the design. [URL] Implement: Browse Redesign.html"
   - "okay, das sieht schon mal sehr gut aus. Lasse allerdings diese Punkte auf den Ordner, die da rechts unten oder links unten in der Ecke sind, weg. Außerdem füge eins zu eins den Chat von Search unten hinzu... Außerdem will ich, wenn man dann zum Beispiel chattet, 'Wo ist X Y?', dass sich dann automatisch der Ordner öffnet und man zu der Datei geleitet wird..."
   - "[screenshot] 1. Erstens: Rechts sollen die Artefakte viel breiter sein... 2. Zweitens: Das Interface unten, also mit dem Chat, soll man in der Höhe verschieben können... Außerdem werde ich jetzt Namen suche für die eindeutig. Es gibt bitte eine Datei. Soll ich dann oben im Dateisystem zu der Datei geleitet werden und nicht irgendwas im Chat angezeigt werden..."
   - "api/search:1 Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error). Daher funktioniert ja auch der chat nicht"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Fix the 500 error on `/api/search`**: The curl tests show the server returns 200. Need to determine why the browser gets 500. Possible causes: Gemini embedding API key missing/invalid, Vite proxy misconfiguration, or server not running. Need to check server logs when the browser makes the request.
   - **Verify AppShell.tsx is in correct state**: The linter kept reverting it. Need to confirm sidebar nav is properly saved.
   - **Complete the paul-web-frontend → main PR**: Was interrupted mid-commit.

8. Current Work:

Immediately before this summary, I was investigating a 500 error on the `/api/search` endpoint. The user reported: `api/search:1 Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error)`.

I ran two diagnostic commands:
1. `curl -s -w "\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/search -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"Anna Müller","k":5}'` → returned HTTP 200 with valid JSON results
2. `grep -n "status\|evidence" /Users/paul/Documents/GitHub/BigBerlinHack/TechEurope/server/src/server/models.py` → confirmed `status` and `evidence` fields exist in FactResponse

The server is running and responding correctly via curl. The 500 must occur in a specific scenario when accessed via the browser/Vite proxy.

Most likely root cause: The Gemini embedding call (`embed_text(query, dimensions=768)`) in `search.py` Stage 1 fails silently (logged as warning, not raised), but if the DB connection or Pydantic validation fails elsewhere it raises 500. Or the Vite dev server at port 5173 isn't running, and the user is accessing the API differently.

9. Optional Next Step:

The user said: "api/search:1 Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error). Daher funktioniert ja auch der chat nicht"

The next step is to check the actual server logs when the browser makes the request (not curl), to identify the exact error. The server logs would show the traceback. I should run:
```bash
# Check recent server logs or run server in foreground to capture the error:
curl -v http://localhost:5173/api/search -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"test","k":5}'
# or check if Vite proxy is configured correctly in vite.config.ts
```
The most likely fix is that the Vite dev server needs to be running (`make dev` or `make web`) for the proxy to work, and the server needs `GEMINI_API_KEY` set in `server/.env` for the embedding stage to not fail.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/paul/.claude/projects/-Users-paul-Documents-GitHub-BigBerlinHack-TechEurope/a3386320-77b2-498f-9435-ab7819673ce4.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

# CODING AGENTS: READ THIS FIRST

This is a **handoff bundle** from Claude Design (claude.ai/design).

A user mocked up designs in HTML/CSS/JS using an AI design tool, then exported this bundle so a coding agent can implement the designs for real.

## What you should do — IMPORTANT

**Read the chat transcripts first.** There are 1 chat transcript(s) in `bigberlin/chats/`. The transcripts show the full back-and-forth between the user and the design assistant — they tell you **what the user actually wants** and **where they landed** after iterating. Don't skip them. The final HTML files are the output, but the chat is where the intent lives.

**Read `bigberlin/project/Browse Redesign.html` in full.** The user had this file open when they triggered the handoff, so it's almost certainly the primary design they want built. Read it top to bottom — don't skim. Then **follow its imports**: open every file it pulls in (shared components, CSS, scripts) so you understand how the pieces fit together before you start implementing.

**If anything is ambiguous, ask the user to confirm before you start implementing.** It's much cheaper to clarify scope up front than to build the wrong thing.

## About the design files

The design medium is **HTML/CSS/JS** — these are prototypes, not production code. Your job is to **recreate them pixel-perfectly** in whatever technology makes sense for the target codebase (React, Vue, native, whatever fits). Match the visual output; don't copy the prototype's internal structure unless it happens to fit.

**Don't render these files in a browser or take screenshots unless the user asks you to.** Everything you need — dimensions, colors, layout rules — is spelled out in the source. Read the HTML and CSS directly; a screenshot won't tell you anything they don't.

## Bundle contents

- `bigberlin/README.md` — this file
- `bigberlin/chats/` — conversation transcripts (read these!)
- `bigberlin/project/` — the `BigBerlin` project files (HTML prototypes, assets, components)

content.content:
1	"""POST /search — 3-Stage Hybrid Search: Semantic ∩ Structural → Rerank.
2	
3	Stage 1 — Semantic: pgvector kNN on inference_embedding (fallback: name_embedding)
4	Stage 2 — Structural: extract entity mentions → 1-hop graph traverse
5	Stage 3 — Combine (intersect if both have hits, union otherwise) + rerank
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import logging
11	import re
12	from datetime import datetime
13	
14	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
15	
16	from server.db import get_db, embed_text
17	from server.models import (
18	    EntityResponse, FactResponse,
19	    SearchRequest, SearchResponse, SearchResult,
20	)
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
23	router = APIRouter(tags=["search"])
24	
25	
26	def _is_single_row_not_found(exc: Exception) -> bool:
27	    text = str(exc)
28	    return "PGRST116" in text or "multiple (or no) rows returned" in text
29	
30	
31	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	# Entity mention extraction (Pioneer stub → naive regex fallback)
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	def _extract_mentions(query: str) -> list[str]:
36	    """Return candidate entity name mentions from a query string.
37	
38	    Pioneer fine-tune is wired here when WS-3 ships (replace this stub).
39	    Fallback: capitalised word sequences (≥2 chars, ≥1 word).
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        from server.extractors.pioneer import extract_mentions  # type: ignore
43	        return extract_mentions(query)
44	    except (ImportError, Exception):
45	        pass
46	
47	    # Simple fallback: grab capitalised tokens or quoted strings
48	    quoted = re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', query)
49	    capitalised = re.findall(r'\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,}(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,})*)\b', query)
50	    return list(dict.fromkeys(quoted + capitalised))  # dedupe, preserve order
51	
52	
53	def _build_entity_response(entity_row: dict, trust_row: dict | None, facts: list[dict]) -> EntityResponse:
54	    fact_responses = [
55	        FactResponse(
56	            id=str(f["id"]),
57	            subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
58	            predicate=f["predicate"],
59	            object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
60	            object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
61	            confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
62	            derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
63	            extraction_method=f.get("extraction_method"),
64	            valid_from=f["valid_from"],
65	            valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
66	            recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
67	            source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
68	        )
69	        for f in facts
70	    ]
71	    return EntityResponse(
72	        id=str(entity_row["id"]),
73	        entity_type=entity_row["entity_type"],
74	        canonical_name=entity_row["canonical_name"],
75	        aliases=entity_row.get("aliases") or [],
76	        attrs=entity_row.get("attrs") or {},
77	        trust_score=float((trust_row or {}).get("trust_score") or 0.0),
78	        fact_count=int((trust_row or {}).get("fact_count") or 0),
79	        source_diversity=int((trust_row or {}).get("source_diversity") or 0),
80	        facts=fact_responses,
81	    )

file.content:
"""POST /search — 3-Stage Hybrid Search: Semantic ∩ Structural → Rerank.

Stage 1 — Semantic: pgvector kNN on inference_embedding (fallback: name_embedding)
Stage 2 — Structural: extract entity mentions → 1-hop graph traverse
Stage 3 — Combine (intersect if both have hits, union otherwise) + rerank
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
from datetime import datetime

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException

from server.db import get_db, embed_text
from server.models import (
    EntityResponse, FactResponse,
    SearchRequest, SearchResponse, SearchResult,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
router = APIRouter(tags=["search"])


def _is_single_row_not_found(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    text = str(exc)
    return "PGRST116" in text or "multiple (or no) rows returned" in text


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity mention extraction (Pioneer stub → naive regex fallback)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _extract_mentions(query: str) -> list[str]:
    """Return candidate entity name mentions from a query string.

    Pioneer fine-tune is wired here when WS-3 ships (replace this stub).
    Fallback: capitalised word sequences (≥2 chars, ≥1 word).
    """
    try:
        from server.extractors.pioneer import extract_mentions  # type: ignore
        return extract_mentions(query)
    except (ImportError, Exception):
        pass

    # Simple fallback: grab capitalised tokens or quoted strings
    quoted = re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', query)
    capitalised = re.findall(r'\b([A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,}(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z]{1,})*)\b', query)
    return list(dict.fromkeys(quoted + capitalised))  # dedupe, preserve order


def _build_entity_response(entity_row: dict, trust_row: dict | None, facts: list[dict]) -> EntityResponse:
    fact_responses = [
        FactResponse(
            id=str(f["id"]),
            subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
            predicate=f["predicate"],
            object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
            object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
            confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
            derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
            extraction_method=f.get("extraction_method"),
            valid_from=f["valid_from"],
            valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
            recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
            source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
        )
        for f in facts
    ]
    return EntityResponse(
        id=str(entity_row["id"]),
        entity_type=entity_row["entity_type"],
        canonical_name=entity_row["canonical_name"],
        aliases=entity_row.get("aliases") or [],
        attrs=entity_row.get("attrs") or {},
        trust_score=float((trust_row or {}).get("trust_score") or 0.0),
        fact_count=int((trust_row or {}).get("fact_count") or 0),
        source_diversity=int((trust_row or {}).get("source_diversity") or 0),
        facts=fact_responses,
    )


def _fetch_entity_with_trust(db, entity_id: str, as_of: datetime | None = None) -> EntityResponse | None:
    try:
        e_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
        entity_row = e_res.data
    except Exception as exc:
        if _is_single_row_not_found(exc):
            return None
        raise
    if not entity_row:
        return None

    facts_q = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("subject_id", entity_id)
    if as_of:
        facts_q = facts_q.lte("valid_from", as_of.isoformat()).or_(
            f"valid_to.is.null,valid_to.gte.{as_of.isoformat()}"
        )
    else:
        facts_q = facts_q.is_("valid_to", "null")
    facts = facts_q.execute().data or []

    try:
        trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
        trust_row = trust_res.data
    except Exception as exc:
        if _is_single_row_not_found(exc):
            trust_row = None
        else:
            raise
    return _build_entity_response(entity_row, trust_row, facts)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hybrid search implementation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def run_hybrid_search(query: str, k: int = 10, as_of: datetime | None = None, entity_type: str | None = None, db=None) -> list[SearchResult]:
    if db is None:
        db = get_db()

    # --- Stage 1: Semantic ---
    semantic_scores: dict[str, float] = {}
    try:
        query_embedding = embed_text(query, dimensions=768)
        rpc_params: dict = {
            "query_embedding": query_embedding,
            "match_threshold": 0.65,
            "match_count": min(k * 2, 40),
            "use_inference_embedding": True,
        }
        sem_res = db.rpc("match_entities", rpc_params).execute()
        for row in sem_res.data or []:
            if entity_type and row.get("entity_type") != entity_type:
                continue
            semantic_scores[str(row["id"])] = float(row["similarity"])
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.warning("Semantic search failed (embedding unavailable?): %s", exc)

    # --- Stage 2: Structural ---
    structural_ids: set[str] = set()
    mentions = _extract_mentions(query)
    for mention in mentions[:5]:  # cap at 5 mentions
        name_res = db.table("entities").select("id").ilike(
            "canonical_name", f"%{mention}%"
        ).limit(3).execute()
        for row in name_res.data or []:
            root_id = str(row["id"])
            structural_ids.add(root_id)
            # 1-hop graph traverse
            hop_res = db.table("facts").select("subject_id, object_id").or_(
                f"subject_id.eq.{root_id},object_id.eq.{root_id}"
            ).is_("valid_to", "null").limit(20).execute()
            for f in hop_res.data or []:
                if f.get("subject_id"):
                    structural_ids.add(str(f["subject_id"]))
                if f.get("object_id"):
                    structural_ids.add(str(f["object_id"]))

    # --- Stage 3: Combine + rerank ---
    if semantic_scores and structural_ids:
        intersection = set(semantic_scores.keys()) & structural_ids
        combined_ids = intersection if intersection else (set(semantic_scores.keys()) | structural_ids)
        match_type = "hybrid"
    elif semantic_scores:
        combined_ids = set(semantic_scores.keys())
        match_type = "semantic"
    else:
        combined_ids = structural_ids
        match_type = "structural"

    results: list[SearchResult] = []
    for entity_id in list(combined_ids)[:k * 2]:
        entity = _fetch_entity_with_trust(db, entity_id, as_of)
        if not entity:
            continue
        if entity_type and entity.entity_type != entity_type:
            continue
        score = semantic_scores.get(entity_id, 0.5)
        # Boost by trust score
        final_score = score * 0.7 + entity.trust_score * 0.3
        results.append(SearchResult(entity=entity, score=final_score, match_type=match_type))

    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.score, reverse=True)
    return results[:k]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Router
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@router.post("/search", response_model=SearchResponse)
def search(req: SearchRequest, db=Depends(get_db)):
    if not req.query.strip():
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Query must not be empty")

    results = run_hybrid_search(
        query=req.query,
        k=req.k,
        as_of=req.as_of,
        entity_type=req.entity_type,
        db=db,
    )
    return SearchResponse(query=req.query, results=results, total=len(results))

file.content:
"""Shared Pydantic models — API responses, MCP outputs, and domain entities.

Two coexisting concerns:

1. **API/MCP response models** (``EntityResponse``, ``FactResponse``, etc.) —
   what WS-4's REST endpoints + MCP tools return.
2. **Domain models** (``SourceRecord``, ``Entity``, ``Fact``, ``Resolution``) —
   the canonical schema from ``docs/data-model.md`` used by connectors,
   resolver, and ingestion. ``ResolutionDecisionKind`` is the StrEnum form;
   ``ResolutionDecision`` (further down) is the API request payload.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime
from enum import StrEnum
from typing import Any

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source attribution (API)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class SourceReference(BaseModel):
    system: str
    path: str | None = None
    record_id: str | None = None
    timestamp: datetime | None = None
    method: str = "unknown"  # "regex_extract", "llm_gemini_2.0", "human_input", …


class EvidenceItem(BaseModel):
    source: str
    record_id: str | None = None
    quote: str | None = None
    field: str | None = None
    confidence: float | None = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facts (API responses)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class FactResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    subject_id: str
    predicate: str
    object_id: str | None = None
    object_literal: Any | None = None
    confidence: float
    derivation: str
    extraction_method: str | None = None
    valid_from: datetime
    valid_to: datetime | None = None
    recorded_at: datetime
    source_id: str
    status: str = "active"
    evidence: list[EvidenceItem] = []


class ProvenanceResponse(BaseModel):
    fact: FactResponse
    source_reference: SourceReference
    superseded_by: FactResponse | None = None
    trust_weight: float = 0.5


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities (API responses)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class EntityResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    entity_type: str
    canonical_name: str

Emp1: Hello Surya, I'm engaged in developing a new feature for one of our upcoming product launches at Inazuma.co. I've come across a crucial line in the codebase that's pivotal to our project timeline.

Emp2: Hi Meghna, could you clarify the significance of this specific line?

Emp1: Certainly, the line is `self.AUTHORIZATION_URL = 'https://jawbone.com/auth/oauth2/auth'`. This URL is essential as it reroutes the user once they click the 'Authorize' button.

Emp2: Understood. However, why aren't we utilizing the `requests` library for handling HTTP requests?

Emp1: I chose to use the `social.utils.handle_http_errors` function instead, as it provides a reliable method to consistently manage HTTP errors and exceptions throughout the project.

Emp2: Got it. Could you elaborate on the function of the `self.SCOPE_SEPARATOR` variable?

Emp1: The `self.SCOPE_SEPARATOR` variable is vital for separating the scopes requested by the user in the authorization URL. For example, it would be `' '`, resulting in an authorization URL like `https://jawbone.com/auth/oauth2/auth?scope=read%20profile`.

Emp2: That makes sense. Could you explain how the `self.REDIRECT_STATE` variable affects the implementation?

Emp1: The `self.REDIRECT_STATE` variable determines whether the redirect URL is stored in the request's state. It's set to `False`, meaning the redirect URL won't be stored in the request's state.

Emp2: What role does the `handle_http_errors` function play?

Emp1: The `handle_http_errors` function is crucial for capturing and managing any HTTP errors during the OAuth2 process. It provides a more robust and consistent error management approach compared to directly making HTTP requests.
